test.command: bash -c 'NVIDIA_BASE="$(python3 -c "import sys,os;print(os.path.join(sys.prefix,\"lib\",f\"python{sys.version_info.major}.{sys.version_info.minor}\",\"site-packages\",\"nvidia\"))" 2>/dev/null||true)" && if [ -d "${NVIDIA_BASE:-}" ]; then for d in "$NVIDIA_BASE"/*/lib; do [ -d "$d" ] && LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$d:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH:-}"; done; export LD_LIBRARY_PATH; fi && clj -M:test'

typecheck.command: N/A — Clojure is dynamically typed, no typecheck configured

highlights: Dead code: infer-keyword-columns defined but unused in parquet.clj
No input validation on embed-batch, cluster-embeddings, write-parquet
Manifest started-at and completed-at always identical (both set at creation)
Hash values use raw hex instead of 'sha256:' prefix per spec §5.1
Dead code in resolve-taxonomy string-key fallbacks (cheshire keywordizes)
No nil guard on public normalize-text function
O(n²) noise-point lookup in splitter (recomputes per record)
Duplicated noise-group logic between group-by-cluster and split-clusters
Memory scalability: build-state atom stores full embeddings
Missing explicit ns requires for clojure.string and clojure.set in core.clj
Misleading docstring about type validation in validate-parquet-schema

appliedUpdates.description: Added HDBSCAN cosine metric algorithm='generic' requirement to python-bridge.md
Added SentenceTransformer model caching and CPU device forcing guidance to python-bridge.md
Added e5 query/passage prefix convention to python-bridge.md
Added parquet keyword-columns tracking convention to python-bridge.md
Added NFKC Unicode normalization notes (½ → 1⁄2 with U+2044) to python-bridge.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Always use clojure.edn/read-string (not clojure.core/read-string) for reading EDN data files. clojure.core/read-string can execute arbitrary code via reader macros (#=).'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: pipeline-stages-2-3 used clojure.core/read-string in core.clj:175 to parse canonical-records.edn. While the file is self-generated, this pattern could spread to other workers. The reviewer and the clojure-dsl-worker skill should flag this.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'All namespaces used in a file MUST be listed in the ns :require form, even when using fully-qualified calls. Do not rely on transitive loading.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: manifest-and-verification worker used clojure.string and clojure.set via fully-qualified calls in verification/core.clj without requiring them. This works due to transitive loading but is fragile.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify hash format convention: Decide whether hash values should use 'sha256:' prefix (as shown in spec §5.1 examples) or raw 64-char hex strings (as implemented). Document the chosen convention.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Spec §5.1 shows ':source-id "sha256:abc123..."' but all implementations (stages.clj, manifest.clj) use raw hex. Multiple features (pipeline-stages-0-1, manifest-and-verification) followed the raw hex pattern. This should be explicitly documented to prevent future confusion.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker skillFeedback suggests documenting LD_LIBRARY_PATH requirement in the clojure-dsl-worker skill
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — This is already captured in .factory/library/python-bridge.md and the services.yaml test command handles it automatically. Skill changes are out of scope for this validator.

rejectedObservations.observation: Manifest timestamps (started-at, completed-at) are always identical
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — Whether distinct timestamps are required is unclear from the spec. The current behavior is functional; changing the API signature is a design decision for the orchestrator. Noted as non-blocking issue but not actionable here.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker followed TDD procedure correctly (observation from manifest-and-verification review)
rejectedObservations.reason: Not actionable — positive observation, no state change needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: init.sh and services.yaml test command duplication for LD_LIBRARY_PATH setup
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — Both serve different purposes (init.sh for interactive setup, services.yaml for automated test running). Deduplication would require design decision about which is authoritative.
